1.content:
About the DAA. It's:
 - Safe: The Dashboards app and the DTR are both restricted to read-only permissions/calls by design. No data ever leaves the Dynatrace tenant.
 - Fast: The analytics happens where the data resides, in Grail™.
 - Open source: Copy, inspect, customize and share it in a few clicks.

access-origins.title: Top Access Origins (IPs)
access-origins.description:
**Description:**  
This table displays the top 15 origin addresses with the highest number of API Gateway audit events triggered from browser sources. It also shows the number of unique users and total hits per address.  

**Purpose:**  
This visualization helps identify high-traffic sources and user activity patterns, enabling better monitoring of API usage and potential anomalies.
access-origins.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize hits = count(), uniqueUsers = countDistinct(userEmail), by:{`origin.address`}
| sort hits desc
| limit 15

activity-heatmap.title: Activity Heatmap (Hour × Day of Week)
activity-heatmap.description:
**Description:**  
This tile visualizes audit events from the API Gateway accessed via browsers, grouped by hour and day of the week. It highlights activity patterns, helping identify peak usage times and trends.  

**Purpose:**  
The honeycomb visualization is ideal for spotting temporal patterns and anomalies in API Gateway usage, enabling better resource planning and issue detection.
activity-heatmap.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| fieldsAdd hour = formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"HH"), dow = formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"EEEE")
| summarize activity = count(), by:{hour, dow}
| sort dow, hour

app-adoption-table.title: App Usage Ranking
app-adoption-table.description:
**Description:**  
This tile displays a table summarizing application usage data for Dynatrace apps accessed via the API Gateway from browsers. It shows the number of visits (`appVisits`) and unique users (`uniqueUsers`) per app (`appName`), sorted by the most visited apps.  

**Purpose:**  
This visualization helps identify the most accessed Dynatrace apps, track user engagement, and monitor API Gateway usage patterns, providing insights into app popularity and user behavior.
app-adoption-table.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter contains(resource, "/app-engine/registry/v1/apps/dynatrace.")
| parse resource, "'/platform/app-engine/registry/v1/apps/' LD:appName"
| filter isNotNull(appName)
| filter NOT contains(appName, "/")
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize appVisits = count(), uniqueUsers = countDistinct(userEmail), by:{appName}
| sort appVisits desc

app-adoption-trend.title: App Usage Over Time
app-adoption-trend.description:
**Description:**  
This tile tracks daily API Gateway audit events originating from browsers, specifically for Dynatrace AppEngine applications. It counts visits per app name, excluding invalid or nested app names.  

**Purpose:**  
The area chart effectively visualizes trends in app usage over time, helping identify patterns, anomalies, or changes in API activity for specific applications.
app-adoption-trend.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter contains(resource, "/app-engine/registry/v1/apps/dynatrace.")
| parse resource, "'/platform/app-engine/registry/v1/apps/' LD:appName"
| filter isNotNull(appName)
| filter NOT contains(appName, "/")
| makeTimeseries visits = count(), by:{appName}, interval:1d

auth-methods.title: Authentication Methods
auth-methods.description:
**Description:**  
This tile visualizes the distribution of authentication types based on audit events, showing the total occurrences and unique users for each type. It combines audit and query execution events to enrich user data, ensuring accurate insights into user activity.

**Purpose:**  
The pie chart effectively highlights the proportional usage of different authentication methods, making it easy to identify trends or anomalies in user authentication behavior.
auth-methods.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize total = count(), uniqueUsers = countDistinct(userEmail), by:{`authentication.type`}
| sort total desc

config-by-schema.title: Config Changes by Schema
config-by-schema.description: Configuration changes grouped by settings schema ID and event type. Shows which configuration areas (alerting, management zones, SLOs, dashboards, etc.) are being actively modified — a detailed governance and audit trail.
config-by-schema.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "SETTINGS"
| filter event.outcome == "success"
| summarize changes = count(), by:{`details.dt.settings.schema_id`, `event.type`}
| sort changes desc
| limit 30

config-by-user.title: Config Changes by User
config-by-user.description:
**Query Description:**  
This query analyzes audit events related to successful settings changes in Dynatrace. It identifies users who made these changes by correlating audit events with query execution events to retrieve user email addresses. The query then summarizes the total number of changes and distinct schemas modified by each user, sorted by the number of changes in descending order.

**Purpose of Visualization:**  
The table visualization provides a clear overview of user activity, showing who made the most changes and the variety of schemas they modified. This is useful for auditing, identifying key contributors, and ensuring accountability in system settings management.
config-by-user.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "SETTINGS"
| filter event.outcome == "success"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize changes = count(), schemas = countDistinct(`details.dt.settings.schema_id`), by:{userEmail}
| sort changes desc

config-change-trend.title: Config Changes Over Time
config-change-trend.description:
**Description for Tile Indicator:**

This tile tracks successful configuration changes in Dynatrace settings over time, grouped by event type. The area chart highlights trends and patterns in daily changes, aiding in audit and compliance monitoring.
config-change-trend.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "SETTINGS"
| filter event.outcome == "success"
| makeTimeseries configChanges = count(), by:{`event.type`}, interval:1d

daily-activity-trend.title: Daily Activity by Source
daily-activity-trend.description:
**Description:**  
This tile visualizes daily counts of audit events from the API Gateway, grouped by the source of the requests. The area chart effectively highlights trends and variations in event volume over time, making it easier to identify patterns or anomalies in request sources.
daily-activity-trend.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| makeTimeseries total = count(), by:{`request.source`}, interval:1d

davis-copilot-usage.title: Davis Copilot Usage Trend
davis-copilot-usage.description:
**Description:**  
This tile tracks daily usage of Davis CoPilot API endpoints by counting audit events from the API Gateway.  

**Purpose:**  
The line chart helps visualize trends in Davis CoPilot usage over time, enabling teams to monitor adoption, detect anomalies, or identify usage patterns.
davis-copilot-usage.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter contains(resource, "/davis/copilot/")
| makeTimeseries davisUsage = count(), interval:1d

depth-header.content:
### 🧭 Feature Exploration & Observability Coverage
How deeply are users exploring the platform? Tracks breadth of apps used and observability domains queried.

dql-query-volume.title: DQL Query Execution Volume
dql-query-volume.description:
**Description:**  
This tile tracks the daily count of audit events from the API Gateway related to query executions (`/storage/query/v1/query:execute`), grouped by the request source.  

**Purpose:**  
The line chart helps visualize trends in query execution activity over time, enabling identification of usage patterns or anomalies across different request sources.
dql-query-volume.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter contains(resource, "/storage/query/v1/query:execute")
| makeTimeseries queries = count(), by:{`request.source`}, interval:1d

engagement-header.content:
### 🏆 User Engagement Scorecard
Per-user adoption depth: sessions, apps explored, queries run, data tables queried, and data scanned.

event-volume-provider.title: Audit Event Volume by Provider
event-volume-provider.description:
**Description:**  
This tile visualizes audit events grouped by provider, showing the total count for each provider in a pie chart. It highlights the distribution of audit events, making it easy to identify the most active providers at a glance.
event-volume-provider.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| summarize total = count(), by:{event.provider}
| sort total desc

failed-events.title: Failed / Error Events
failed-events.description:
**Tile Description:**  
"Displays the top 20 audit event failures grouped by provider and outcome, sorted by frequency. Useful for identifying and prioritizing the most common failure types in audit logs."
failed-events.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.outcome != "success" and event.outcome != "200" and event.outcome != "201" and event.outcome != "204"
| summarize failures = count(), by:{event.provider, event.outcome}
| sort failures desc
| limit 20

feature-depth-per-user.title: Apps Explored per User (dt.app.id)
feature-depth-per-user.description:
**Description:**  
This tile displays a table summarizing user interactions with applications accessed via the API Gateway from browsers. It shows the number of distinct applications explored (`appsExplored`) and the total interactions (`totalInteractions`) for each user, identified by their email or user ID.  

**Purpose:**  
This visualization is useful for analyzing user behavior, identifying active users, and understanding application usage patterns. It helps track engagement and detect trends in API Gateway interactions.
feature-depth-per-user.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter isNotNull(`dt.app.id`)
| filter `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.appshell" and `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.launcher"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize appsExplored = countDistinct(`dt.app.id`), totalInteractions = count(), by:{userEmail}
| sort appsExplored desc
| limit 100

footer.content:
---

<center>

**Built with ❤️ by the Dynatrace Customer Success Team**

*This dashboard is maintained as part of the India CS adoption tracking initiative.*
*Data is sourced from Dynatrace Grail audit events and query execution telemetry.*

📧 For questions, feedback, or feature requests, please create issue in GitHub repo: [CustomerSuccess](https://github.com/dynatrace-oss/CustomerSuccess)

</center>

grail-data-scanned-trend.title: Grail Data Scanned Over Time (GB)
grail-data-scanned-trend.description:
**Description:**  
This tile visualizes the daily sum of scanned data (in GB) for query execution events, grouped by user email, excluding specific internal domains. The area chart effectively highlights trends in data usage over time, making it easier to identify patterns or anomalies in user activity.
grail-data-scanned-trend.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com")
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@service.sso.dynatrace.com")
| filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@")
| makeTimeseries scannedGB = sum(toDouble(`scanned_bytes`) / 1073741824), by:{`user.email`}, interval:1d

header.content:
## 📊 Dynatrace Adoption & Audit Dashboard
Comprehensive view of user activity, platform adoption, app usage, and configuration changes.

kpi-browser-sessions.title: Browser Interactions
kpi-browser-sessions.description:
**Description:**  
This tile displays the total count of browser-based actions recorded as audit events from the API Gateway. It helps monitor user interactions originating from browsers for security and compliance analysis.
kpi-browser-sessions.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| summarize browserActions = count()

kpi-config-changes.title: Config Changes
kpi-config-changes.description:
**Description for Tile:**  
"Displays the total count of successful configuration changes made via the Settings provider, based on audit events. Useful for tracking and validating system changes over time."
kpi-config-changes.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "SETTINGS"
| filter event.outcome == "success"
| summarize configChanges = count()

kpi-total-events.title: Total Audit Events
kpi-total-events.description: **Description:** This tile displays the total count of audit events (`AUDIT_EVENT`) in the system. It provides a quick overview of audit activity, helping to monitor and track system changes or user actions effectively.
kpi-total-events.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| summarize totalEvents = count()

kpi-unique-users.title: Unique Users
kpi-unique-users.description:
**Query Explanation:**  
This query identifies the number of unique users who triggered audit events (`AUDIT_EVENT`) in the system, excluding system and unknown users. It cross-references these users with those who executed queries (`QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT`) in the last 30 days, filtering out specific email domains. The result is the count of distinct user emails meeting these criteria.

**Purpose and Usefulness:**  
This visualization provides a concise view of active, identifiable users performing both audit and query-related activities, helping to monitor user engagement and detect anomalies in system usage.
kpi-unique-users.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter `user.id` != "UNKNOWN" and `user.id` != "system"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@service.sso.dynatrace.com") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| filter isNotNull(`lu.email`)
| summarize uniqueUsers = countDistinct(`lu.email`)

observability-domain-coverage.title: Observability Domain Coverage
observability-domain-coverage.description:
**Description:**  
This tile tracks the daily count of query execution events grouped by table, excluding specific system users. The area chart effectively visualizes trends and patterns in table usage over time, helping identify high-activity tables or anomalies in query execution.
observability-domain-coverage.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com")
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@service.sso.dynatrace.com")
| filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@")
| filter isNotNull(`table`) and `table` != ""
| makeTimeseries queries = count(), by:{`table`}, interval:1d

platform-area-usage.title: Platform Area Usage
platform-area-usage.description:
**Description:**  
This tile visualizes the distribution of audit events from the API Gateway, filtered for browser-based requests, grouped by platform area. It also highlights the number of visits and unique users per platform area.  

**Purpose:**  
The pie chart effectively shows the proportional usage of different platform areas, helping identify the most accessed areas and user engagement patterns.
platform-area-usage.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| parse resource, "'/platform/' WORD:platformArea '/' LD"
| filter isNotNull(platformArea)
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize visits = count(), uniqueUsers = countDistinct(userEmail), by:{platformArea}
| sort visits desc

query-analytics-header.content:
### 🔬 Grail Query Analytics
What data are users querying? Tracks DQL query patterns, data tables accessed, and per-user query behavior.

query-by-table.title: Queries by Data Table
query-by-table.description:
**Description:**  
This tile displays a summary of query execution events grouped by table. It shows the total queries, unique users, average execution duration (ms), and total data scanned (GB).  

**Purpose:**  
This visualization helps identify the most queried tables, assess query performance, and monitor data usage, enabling optimization of resource consumption and user activity analysis.
query-by-table.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com")
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@service.sso.dynatrace.com")
| summarize queries = count(), uniqueUsers = countDistinct(`user.email`), avgDurationMs = round(avg(toDouble(`execution_duration_ms`)), decimals:1), scannedGB = round(sum(toDouble(`scanned_bytes`)) / 1073741824, decimals:3), by:{`table`}
| sort queries desc

query-by-user.title: Query Activity per User
query-by-user.description:
**Description:**  
This tile displays a summary of query execution events by user email, including total queries, distinct tables queried, average execution duration, data scanned, failed queries, and success rate.  

**Purpose:**  
The table visualization is ideal for analyzing user-specific query performance, identifying inefficiencies, and monitoring query success rates to optimize system usage and troubleshoot failures effectively.
query-by-user.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com")
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@service.sso.dynatrace.com")
| filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@")
| summarize queries = count(), tablesQueried = countDistinct(`table`), avgDurationMs = round(avg(toDouble(`execution_duration_ms`)), decimals:1), scannedGB = round(sum(toDouble(`scanned_bytes`)) / 1073741824, decimals:2), failedQueries = countIf(status == "FAILED"), by:{`user.email`}
| fieldsAdd successRate = round(100.0 * toDouble(queries - failedQueries) / toDouble(queries), decimals:1)
| sort queries desc

query-failure-rate.title: Query Success vs Failure Trend
query-failure-rate.description:
**Description:**  
This tile tracks the daily count of successful and failed query execution events, excluding specific system users. The line chart helps visualize trends over time, enabling quick identification of anomalies or patterns in query execution success and failure rates.
query-failure-rate.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com")
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@service.sso.dynatrace.com")
| makeTimeseries succeeded = countIf(status == "SUCCEEDED"), failed = countIf(status == "FAILED"), interval:1d

real-app-header.content:
### 🎯 App Usage (by dt.app.id)
Direct app-level telemetry from browser sessions — the most accurate adoption signal.

real-app-trend.title: App Usage Trend (dt.app.id)
real-app-trend.description:
**Description:**  
This tile visualizes daily visit counts (`visits`) to specific Dynatrace apps (`dt.app.id`) accessed via a browser, excluding internal apps like `dynatrace.appshell` and `dynatrace.launcher`.  

**Purpose:**  
The area chart highlights trends and fluctuations in app usage over time, making it easy to identify patterns, peak usage periods, or anomalies in browser-based access.
real-app-trend.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter isNotNull(`dt.app.id`)
| filter `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.appshell" and `dt.app.id` != "dynatrace.launcher"
| makeTimeseries visits = count(), by:{`dt.app.id`}, interval:1d

real-app-usage.title: App Usage Ranking (dt.app.id)
real-app-usage.description:
**Description:**  
This table summarizes application usage by aggregating audit events from API Gateway requests originating from browsers. It calculates the total page views, unique users, and sessions per application (`dt.app.id`). Additionally, it enriches user data by linking user IDs to email addresses from query execution events.

**Purpose:**  
This visualization is useful for analyzing application engagement, identifying active users, and understanding session patterns, helping teams monitor user activity and application performance effectively.
real-app-usage.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| filter isNotNull(`dt.app.id`)
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize pageViews = count(), uniqueUsers = countDistinct(userEmail), sessions = countDistinct(`origin.session`), by:{`dt.app.id`}
| sort pageViews desc

top-endpoints.title: Top 20 Most Visited Endpoints
top-endpoints.description:
**Description:**  
This query identifies the top 20 resources accessed via browser through the API Gateway, counting total hits and unique users (based on email or user ID) over the last 30 days.  

**Purpose:**  
The table visualization helps analyze resource usage patterns, detect high-traffic endpoints, and understand user engagement, aiding in performance optimization and security monitoring.
top-endpoints.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize hits = count(), uniqueUsers = countDistinct(userEmail), by:{resource}
| sort hits desc
| limit 20

top-users.title: Top 20 Most Active Users
top-users.description:
**Description:**  
This tile displays the top 20 users (identified by email) who performed the most actions via a browser on the API Gateway. It also shows the total number of actions and the number of active days for each user over the analyzed period.  

**Purpose:**  
This visualization is useful for identifying the most active users, understanding usage patterns, and monitoring engagement with the API Gateway. It helps in auditing and optimizing user interactions.
top-users.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize totalActions = count(), activeDays = countDistinct(formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"yyyy-MM-dd")), by:{userEmail}
| sort totalActions desc
| limit 20

unique-users-trend.title: Unique Users Per Day
unique-users-trend.description:
**Description:**  
This tile tracks the daily count of unique users interacting with the API Gateway via a browser. It correlates audit events with user email data from query execution events over the past 30 days.

**Purpose:**  
The line chart helps visualize user activity trends over time, enabling analysis of API usage patterns and identifying anomalies or changes in user behavior.
unique-users-trend.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| makeTimeseries uniqueUsers = countDistinct(userEmail), interval:1d

user-engagement-scorecard.title: User Engagement Scorecard
user-engagement-scorecard.description:
**Query Explanation:**  
This query analyzes user activity and behavior by combining audit events (`AUDIT_EVENT`) from API Gateway and query execution events (`QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT`) over the last 30 days. It calculates metrics such as browser actions, sessions, active days, and apps used per user. Additionally, it enriches the data with query execution details like the number of queries, tables queried, and data scanned in gigabytes, associating them with user emails.

**Purpose of Visualization:**  
The table visualization provides a comprehensive overview of user activity and resource usage. It helps identify active users, their interaction patterns, and their impact on system resources, enabling better monitoring, optimization, and accountability.
user-engagement-scorecard.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "AUDIT_EVENT"
| filter event.provider == "API_GATEWAY"
| filter `request.source` == "BROWSER"
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | summarize email = first(`user.email`), by: {`user.id`}], sourceField:`user.id`, lookupField:`user.id`, prefix:"lu."
| fieldsAdd userEmail = coalesce(`lu.email`, `user.id`)
| summarize browserActions = count(), sessions = countDistinct(`origin.session`), activeDays = countDistinct(formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"yyyy-MM-dd")), appsUsed = countDistinct(`dt.app.id`), by:{userEmail}
| lookup [fetch dt.system.events, from: now()-30d | filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT" | filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@") | filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com") | summarize queries = count(), tablesQueried = countDistinct(`table`), scannedGB = sum(toDouble(`scanned_bytes`)) / 1073741824, by: {`user.email`}], sourceField:userEmail, lookupField:`user.email`, prefix:"q."
| fieldsAdd queries = toLong(`q.queries`), tablesQueried = toLong(`q.tablesQueried`), scannedGB = round(toDouble(`q.scannedGB`), decimals:2)
| fields userEmail, activeDays, sessions, browserActions, appsUsed, queries, tablesQueried, scannedGB
| sort browserActions desc

weekly-header.content:
### 📅 Weekly User Adoption Trend
Week-over-week breakdown of each user's query volume, data sources explored, and Grail data consumption.

weekly-user-adoption.title: Weekly User Adoption (Queries, Tables, Data Scanned)
weekly-user-adoption.description:
**Description:**  
This table displays weekly query execution metrics per user, including the number of queries executed, distinct tables queried, and total data scanned (in GB). It excludes system and service accounts, focusing on individual user activity.  

**Purpose:**  
This visualization helps identify active users, their query patterns, and resource consumption trends, enabling better monitoring of usage and optimization of query performance.
weekly-user-adoption.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "QUERY_EXECUTION_EVENT"
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@heimdall.dynatrace.com")
| filter NOT contains(toString(`user.email`), "@service.sso.dynatrace.com")
| filter contains(toString(`user.email`), "@")
| fieldsAdd week = formatTimestamp(timestamp, format:"yyyy-'W'ww")
| summarize queries = count(), tablesQueried = countDistinct(`table`), scannedGB = round(sum(toDouble(`scanned_bytes`)) / 1073741824, decimals:2), by:{`user.email`, week}
| sort week desc, queries desc

workflow-header.content:
### 🤖 Workflow Automation Adoption
Automation health and execution patterns — key indicator of platform maturity.

workflow-health.title: Workflow Health (Success Rate)
workflow-health.description:
**Markdown for Tile Description:**

```markdown
Displays the top 15 workflows by execution count, showing total executions, successes, failures, and success rate. Useful for identifying workflow performance and success trends.
```
workflow-health.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "WORKFLOW_EVENT"
| filter event.type == "ACTION_EXECUTION"
| filter `dt.automation_engine.state.is_final` == true
| summarize executions = count(), successes = countIf(`dt.automation_engine.state` == "SUCCESS"), failures = countIf(`dt.automation_engine.state` != "SUCCESS"), by:{`dt.automation_engine.workflow.title`}
| fieldsAdd successRate = round(100.0 * toDouble(successes) / toDouble(executions), decimals:1)
| sort executions desc
| limit 15

workflow-trend.title: Workflow Executions Over Time
workflow-trend.description:
**Description:**  
This tile visualizes the daily count of finalized workflow action executions, grouped by their final states, using an area chart.  

**Purpose:**  
The area chart effectively highlights trends and patterns in workflow states over time, helping identify anomalies or shifts in automation outcomes.
workflow-trend.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.kind == "WORKFLOW_EVENT"
| filter event.type == "ACTION_EXECUTION"
| filter `dt.automation_engine.state.is_final` == true
| makeTimeseries total = count(), by:{`dt.automation_engine.state`}, interval:1d
